metadata.title: US automakers — Ford, GM, Stellantis, Tesla, Rivian, Lucid: EV losses, Cruise wind-down, the VW/Amazon/PIF backers, and NACS (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the makers, the GM/Cruise wind-down, the Stellantis Tavares ouster, the VW-Rivian JV, the Amazon/PIF stakes, Tesla's NACS win + robotaxi are FACT (company filings, news of record); EV-strategy and Tesla-brand readings are presented BOTH ways with dates
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the auto epic (#177). The US makers: the Detroit incumbents (Ford, GM, Stellantis), the EV-natives (Tesla, Rivian, Lucid), and their foreign/Big-Tech backers (VW into Rivian, Amazon into Rivian, Saudi PIF into Lucid). Threads: heavy EV losses at the incumbents, GM's Cruise robotaxi WIND-DOWN, Stellantis's leadership blow-up, Tesla's NACS charging-standard win + robotaxi + the Musk-politics brand overhang. Political/labor threads (UAW, dealer-franchise laws, IRA credits) are mapped in #183. Attaches to Tesla, Amazon, VW, PIF, NACS, US_Auto_Makers.

the_incumbents: Detroit's 'Big Three': FORD (strong ICE/hybrid + Pro commercial; its Model e EV unit lost ~$5B/yr, prompting 2024-25 EV pullbacks/retiming), GENERAL MOTORS (Ultium battery platform; BrightDrop vans; Super Cruise), and STELLANTIS (14 brands incl. Jeep/Ram/Dodge/Chrysler + the EU brands) — whose CEO Carlos Tavares was OUSTED (1 Dec 2024) amid a US inventory glut, dealer revolt, and sales slump; Antonio Filosa became CEO (2025). grade: fact.
the_cruise_winddown: GM's robotaxi bet collapsed: after an Oct-2023 incident in which a Cruise vehicle dragged a pedestrian (and California suspended its permits), GM cut funding and in Dec 2024 announced it would WIND DOWN Cruise as a robotaxi business, folding the tech into GM for personal-vehicle driver-assist. grade: fact.
the_ev_natives: The EV-natives + their backers: TESLA (the volume EV leader; Cybertruck; FSD; launched a robotaxi service in Austin, 2025) — see NACS + Musk below; RIVIAN (R1T/R1S + the coming R2/R3) — Amazon is its largest shareholder and EDV electric-van customer, and Volkswagen Group formed a JV (announced 2024, up to ~$5.8B) to use Rivian's software/electrical architecture; LUCID (Gravity SUV) — majority-backed by Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF, ~60%), high cash burn / low volume. grade: fact.
the_nacs_and_musk: Tesla's standard win: Tesla opened its connector as the North American Charging Standard (NACS), and in 2023-24 Ford, GM, and nearly all other US makers ADOPTED NACS for future EVs + Supercharger access — a rare case of a proprietary design becoming the industry standard. The overhang: Elon Musk's political activity (incl. the 2025 DOGE role) is widely linked to Tesla brand damage + a 2025 European/US sales softening — recorded both ways (a real reputational/sales effect vs Tesla's structural EV lead). grade: NACS adoption fact; the Musk-brand effect is documented but its magnitude is contested.
the_two_sides: Both ways, dated: (A) TRANSITION-WINNERS framing — Tesla leads EVs + set the charging standard; Ford/GM are pacing EV to demand while milking profitable trucks/hybrids; foreign + Big-Tech capital (VW, Amazon, PIF) is funding the US EV-natives. (B) STRESS framing — incumbent EV units lose billions, GM's robotaxi bet failed, Stellantis imploded at the top, Lucid/Rivian burn cash, and Tesla faces a Musk-driven brand/sales risk + slowing EV growth + the 2025 IRA-credit rollback. grade: both documented.
the_honest_reading: The US auto map is a split screen: Tesla's genuine EV + charging-standard (NACS) lead and the EV-natives' marquee backers (VW, Amazon, PIF) on one side; billions in incumbent EV losses, GM's failed Cruise robotaxi, Stellantis's leadership blow-up, and Tesla's Musk-politics brand risk on the other. The dated facts (Tavares out Dec-2024, Cruise wound down Dec-2024, VW-Rivian JV 2024, NACS adoption 2023-24, Tesla Austin robotaxi 2025) are clear; the EV-strategy and Musk-brand readings are presented both ways. Political/labor threads continue in #183. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Reuters — GM to wind down Cruise robotaxi business (Dec 2024)
Reuters — Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares resigns/ousted (Dec 2024)
Volkswagen Group — Rivian-VW software joint venture (up to ~$5.8B, 2024)
Reuters — Lucid and Saudi Arabia's PIF (majority backer)
The Verge — Ford adopts Tesla's NACS charging standard (the dominoes, 2023)
AP — Tesla launches robotaxi service in Austin (2025)
